sections.heading: Autonomous Systems
sections.body: An autonomous system operates and makes decisions independently, without requiring continuous human control — building on the intelligent-agent concepts from Unit 1, but emphasizing systems that must operate reliably over extended periods with minimal supervision.

sections.heading: Robotics
sections.body: Robotics combines AI's perception, planning, and decision-making with physical hardware, letting autonomous systems sense and act in the physical world — from industrial arms to mobile robots navigating unpredictable real environments.

sections.heading: Self-driving Systems
sections.body: Self-driving systems are a prominent application of autonomous agents, integrating perception (interpreting sensor data), decision-making (choosing safe actions), and control (executing steering/braking) — all under strict real-time and safety constraints.

sections.heading: Intelligent Decision Making
sections.body: Autonomous agents must make decisions continuously as new information arrives, often under the same uncertainty and incomplete-knowledge challenges covered earlier in this course — drawing on planning, learning, and probabilistic reasoning together.

sections.heading: Agent Communication
sections.body: When multiple autonomous agents operate in the same environment (e.g. multiple self-driving vehicles), communication protocols let them share intentions and coordinate — connecting back to the multi-agent systems concepts covered earlier in this unit.

quiz.question: An autonomous system is defined by its ability to:
options: Require continuous human control for every decision
Operate and make decisions independently with minimal supervision
Only work in simulation, never the real world

quiz.question: Self-driving systems integrate which capabilities?
options: Perception, decision-making, and control, under real-time safety constraints

quiz.question: Why is agent communication important for multiple autonomous agents sharing an environment?
options: It lets agents share intentions and coordinate to avoid conflicts
It replaces the need for sensors
It only matters for a single agent
